text: watching my new tweet scheduler post its first live tweet from a json queue. cost so far, roughly one cent. next step, wire the runner into github actions so my laptop stops being the bottleneck. #buildinpublic #indiehackers #devtools

X API gotcha for anyone setting up posting. changing app permissions to read+write does not update your existing access tokens. they keep the OLD scope. regenerate the access token after every permission change or posting returns 403. #buildinpublic #devtools #coding

tweets: Saw a LinkedIn post from Krinal Mehta today claiming news and media publishers are seeing traffic drops of up to 38 percent from AI driven search changes. #SEO #AIOverviews #Publishing

Caveat. This is one practitioner's observation, not an official Google number. That said, multiple publishers have reported similar patterns over the last few months, so the trend looks real even if the exact figure varies.

text: Google now auto generates Q and A on Business Profiles using your reviews and web data. You approve before it goes live. If you do local SEO, stop ignoring the Q and A section. It just became a ranking input, not a formality. #LocalSEO #GoogleBusinessProfile #SEO

tweets: New LocalMighty report, covered on Business Insider, says local SEO outcomes now depend on integrated AI and multi platform discovery, not just Google rankings. #LocalSEO #AI #SEO

Caveat. This is vendor framed research, so read with that lens. That said, the direction matches what most local SEOs are seeing day to day. Ranking in the 3 pack alone is no longer the win it used to be.
Edward Sturm published a test on YouTube claiming FAQ schema no longer moves the needle for SEO. His data shows no meaningful ranking or CTR lift. #SEO #SchemaMarkup #TechnicalSEO

Caveat. This is a single test from one creator, not a large scale study. Still, it lines up with what many have seen since Google rolled back FAQ rich results in 2023. Worth retesting on your own pages before deciding.

text: Shift in Google's local algorithm worth knowing. Brand prominence matters less. Profile interactions matter more. Photo views, review reads, Q and A clicks, website visits. Engagement beats reputation now. #LocalSEO #SEO #GoogleMaps

Fresh data point on local SEO citations in 2026. Five citations from authoritative industry directories rank better than 30 citations from low quality local directories. Quality beats quantity, still. #LocalSEO #SEO #Citations

Google Business Profile now ships scheduling and multi location publishing for Posts. If you run a chain or multi location brand, this is a real workflow upgrade. #LocalSEO #GoogleBusinessProfile #SmallBusiness

Uncomfortable 2026 local SEO truth. Google's AI Overviews pull from Business Profiles and favor professional photography. iPhone photos get skipped. Your camera roll is now an SEO input. #LocalSEO #SEO #AIOverviews

tweets: Heard someone say recently that Google doesn't care about FAQ schema anymore. My take, keep adding it. Two reasons below. #SEO #LocalSEO
Reason one. Don't drink the Google Kool Aid. Google has told us to go left a hundred times and then quietly turned right. Their public stance and what their algo actually rewards often disagree.

Reason two. Future proofing. If Google flips in a year or two and starts weighting FAQ schema again, you're staring at hundreds of URLs that need backfilling. Weeks of work to save a few minutes today.
Also, it's bigger than Google now. Perplexity, ChatGPT, Gemini, they all parse structured data. Schema isn't a Google thing anymore, it's an ecosystem thing.

Nobody really knows how the search ecosystem evolves from here. Betting that structured data stops mattering feels like the wrong side of history. Ship the schema. #SchemaMarkup #AISearch #buildinpublic #SaaS

Stop trusting Claude Code. Seriously. Hot take, but hear me out. Building a local SEO SaaS in Rails for months, Claude Code is a real unlock. But nobody talks enough about how it quietly ignores your instructions with full confidence. #AI #buildinpublic
Not hallucinations. Not made up APIs. The subtle stuff. I tell it a specific date format. Diff looks perfect. Tests green. A week later half the CSV exports are in a different format. #AIcoding #SaaS
I put never use double hyphens in my project instructions. Explicit. Loaded into every single session. Three days later, double hyphens littered across my commit messages. #AIcoding #devtools
I give it a precise spec for queue backpressure. I get back close enough. In a way that only breaks under real load. Cool. Love that for me. #buildinpublic #SaaS

This isn't Claude being dumb. It's Claude being averaged. The model reaches for the common pattern instead of your specific one. Its confidence is identical either way. That's the dangerous part. #AI #LLM
What actually saved me. Read every diff like the thing never saw your instructions. Don't ask did you do X. Ask show me where you did X. #AIcoding #devtools
Run it end to end. Tests lie. Tests really lie. If a change looks right but feels a little lucky, it's almost always wrong. #buildinpublic #AIcoding

Treat Claude Code like a genuinely talented junior engineer who's slightly too sure of themselves. Pair with it, don't autopilot with it. The speed is real. The drift is also real. Trust but verify. Every. Single. Time. #AIcoding #buildinpublic #SaaS #devtools

text:
24 hours in SEO:

Google says do structured data.
Danny Sullivan calls commodity content dead.
Manual action on 850K AI articles.
Selective indexing is the new floor.

AI didn't kill SEO. It raised the bar at every gate.

#SEO #GEO #AISearch #GoogleUpdate

Ran rank checks across 500 GBPs in three cities. The variance inside a single 5-mile radius is wild. A business can be #1 at its own front door and #14 four blocks away. One pin on a map is a lie. #LocalSEO #SEO

Google quietly banned two things in GBP reviews this month. Staff review quotas. Asking customers to mention employees by name. Both are now explicit Rating Manipulation violations with Gemini enforcement live. If your agency is still doing either, stop today. #LocalSEO #SEO

The GBP API now exposes customer review images as structured data. First time. Every serious local SEO tool is rebuilding their review module this week. The bar for what review intelligence looks like just moved. #LocalSEO #SEO #devtools

Built for 11 weeks before I called a single local SEO. The first call killed two features I was halfway through. Cost me a month. If you are shipping in silence, the talking to users tax is cheaper than the rebuild tax. #buildinpublic #SaaS

Half the timeline is GEO is the new SEO. It is not. ChatGPT, Perplexity, AI Overviews. They all reward the same fundamentals. Reviews, GBP categories, citations, fresh content. The acronym changes, the work does not. #SEO #LocalSEO #GEO

Spent four hours debugging why my geo-grid scan returned coordinates in the Atlantic Ocean for a Dallas plumber. Lat and lng swapped. Again. Bookmarking this for the next time I think I am clever. #buildinpublic #devtools

6 percent of consumers used ChatGPT to find local businesses in 2025. 45 percent in 2026. If your local SEO playbook still ends at the map pack, you are optimizing for a smaller and smaller pie. #LocalSEO #GEO #SEO

Cross checked AI Overview citations against map pack winners for thirty local service queries. The cited GBPs were not always the top three. Different surface, different signals. Optimizing for one does not get you the other for free. #LocalSEO #GEO #SEO

Ask Maps lets users describe a situation instead of typing keywords. Quiet enough for a date. Dog friendly patio with shade. Schema and reviews now have to answer questions, not list features. The optimization unit is the use case, not the keyword. #LocalSEO #SEO #GEO

Search ranking volatility hit 9.5 out of 10 in April. Home services took the biggest hit. If your HVAC or plumbing client is panicking about traffic this week, it is not just them. Pull the volatility chart, screenshot it, send it. #LocalSEO #SEO
Most local SEO audits are someone running a crawler and pasting the output into a PDF. That is not an audit, it is a screenshot with a logo. The work is the diagnosis, not the export. #LocalSEO #SEO

Building solo. Server bill last month, 38 dollars. Revenue, zero. Active beta users, eight. The follower count is not the scoreboard. The eight people in my DMs telling me what is broken are. #buildinpublic #SaaS
A GEO agency that cannot audit your GBP, your review velocity, your category accuracy is a copywriter with a buzzword. The retrieval model changed. The plumbing under it did not. #SEO #GEO #LocalSEO

Shipped a smart geocoder fallback that silently mislabeled six percent of pins for two days. No errors, no alerts, just wrong data. New rule. If a fallback can be wrong, it has to be loud. #buildinpublic #devtools

Half the build in public crowd is going quiet right now. Burnout, mismatched expectations, fading algo love. The version that lasts is honest progress, slower cadence, fewer hot takes. Marathon, not a launch week. #buildinpublic #SaaS
Unpopular opinion. Local SEO agencies that show clients one ranking number from one location are hiding the picture. The map pack is not a single position, it is a heat map. #LocalSEO #SEO

Refactored the grid scan loop. 3.1 seconds per scan down to 380ms. Same data, fewer API calls, batched smarter. The boring infra work nobody tweets about is what makes the product not embarrassing to demo. #buildinpublic #SaaS

Pattern across roughly 200 GBPs I have crawled. Profiles with photos updated in the last 30 days consistently rank better in the map pack than those with stale media. Freshness shows up in places people do not look. #LocalSEO #SEO
Every AI search is killing SEO thread is written by someone selling an AI search course. The fundamentals did not get killed. They got renamed. #SEO #GEO #LocalSEO
